response:
# Perspective Audit — Chat Interface With Cognitive Load Issues

## Step 1 — Escalated perspectives confirmed

Running deep JTBD checklists for the perspectives the a11y-critic escalated to MEDIUM/HIGH:

- **Cognitive & Neurodivergent — HIGH**
- **Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity — MEDIUM**
- **Keyboard & Motor Access — MEDIUM**

**Not reviewed (rated LOW by the critic — checklists not run per protocol):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Auditory Access, Screen Reader & Semantic Structure.

Two color-only / semantic observations fell at the boundary of a LOW perspective during review (the new-message color flash → 1.4.1; `role="grid"` without grid behavior → 4.1.2). I did **not** run those checklists; I log them in Open Questions as possible re-escalation signals rather than scoring them here.

---

## Step 4 — Per-perspective checklists

### Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent (HIGH)

| JTBD item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Error messages suggest specific corrective action (3.3.3) | N/A | No validation errors surfaced. `sendMessage` silently no-ops on empty input (`if (!inputValue.trim()) return;`, ~L224) — a silent no-op with no feedback, but no error-message SC applies. Noted, not scored. |
| Multi-step form does not re-ask earlier data (3.3.7) | N/A | No multi-step form. |
| Auth allows paste/autofill, no puzzle (3.3.8) | N/A | No authentication. |
| Navigation same relative position across pages (3.2.3) | N/A | Single component, not a multi-page flow. |
| Same-function components have consistent labels (3.2.4) | N/A | Single component. |
| Help mechanism in same location (3.2.6) | N/A | No help mechanism. |
| Focus does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1) | **PASS** | Emoji insertion is bound to `onClick` (~L322), not focus. Focusing an emoji does nothing — correctly avoids the on-focus trap. |
| Changing a control value does not auto-submit (3.2.2) | **PASS** | `textarea onChange` only updates state (~L336). Enter-to-send is a deliberate keypress and a documented chat convention, not an on-input context change. |
| Destructive actions have undo/confirm (3.3.4) | N/A | No destructive actions. |
| More than one way to reach a page (2.4.5) | N/A | Single component. |
| Personal-data inputs have `autocomplete` (1.3.5) | N/A | The one field is a free-text message composer, not identifiable personal data. |
| Timeout warnings give ≥20s (2.2.1) | N/A | No session time limit. (Timestamp auto-update is 2.2.2, not 2.2.1 — see below.) |
| Content avoids unexplained jargon/complex sentences | **PASS** | Copy is plain. |
| **Neurodivergent:** layout predictable, no layout shifts | **FINDING** | Auto-scroll yanks the viewport on every message and timestamps mutate every 60s — the surface is not stable while reading. Detailed as **F1** (auto-scroll, cross-cutting) and **F2** (timestamps). |
| **Neurodivergent:** visual hierarchy clear | **PASS** | Message bubbles, alignment, and timestamps group the reading order adequately. |
| **Neurodivergent:** no autoplay media / unprompted modals | **PASS** | Emoji picker opens only on user click (~L308); no modal auto-appears; no autoplay media. |
| **Neurodivergent:** instructions don't rely on memory | **PASS** | No step-dependent instructions. |

**Red flags checked:** CAPTCHA/cognitive test — none. Paste disabled — none. Multi-step re-collection — none. Destructive action w/o confirm — none. **No cognitive red flag fires.**

---

### Perspective 3 — Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity (MEDIUM)

| JTBD item | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Non-essential animations suppressed under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` | **FINDING** | No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion)` anywhere in the stylesheet (absence noted at ~L94). `bounce` (infinite, `.typing-dot`, ~L86–92) and `flashBg` (`.new-message-flash`, ~L99–106) run unconditionally. → **F1** |
| Essential animations reduced to opacity/fade under reduced-motion | **FINDING** | No reduced-motion branch exists to fall back to. → **F1** |
| Auto-updating/moving content >5s has pause/stop/hide control (2.2.2) | **FINDING** | `scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth'})` fires on every `messages` change (~L212–214) with no scroll-lock; recurs indefinitely in an active chat. Timestamps auto-update indefinitely (see F2). No pause affordance. → **F1** (scroll), **F2** (timestamps) |
| No content flashes >3×/second (2.3.1) | **PASS** | `flashBg` is a single 0.4s fade (~L103–106); `bounce` is 0.6s motion, not luminance flashing. No seizure risk. |
| Device-motion functionality has UI alternative (2.5.4) | N/A | No device-motion triggers. |
| Parallax removed/disabled under reduced-motion | N/A | No parallax. |
| Video auto-play disabled/controlled | N/A | No video. |

**Red flags checked:**
- Flashing >3/s — **does not fire** (single fade).
- **`prefers-reduced-motion` media query absent when animations exist — FIRES.** Multiple animations (`bounce`, `flashBg`) plus a recurring smooth auto-scroll, zero reduced-motion handling. Per protocol this is auto-CRITICAL → **F1**.
- No pause on auto-playing carousel/video — no carousel/video; the equivalent auto-scroll/timestamp pause gap is captured under 2.2.2 above.

---

### Perspective 5 — Keyboard & Motor Access (MEDIUM)

| JTBD item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| All interactive elements reachable & operable by keyboard (2.1.1) | **PASS** | Emoji toggle, textarea, Send, and all 64 emoji are native `<button>`/`<textarea>` — each is tabbable and Enter/Space-operable. Operability is met; **efficiency** is not (see F3). |
| No keyboard trap (2.1.2) | **PASS** | The picker is non-modal; Tab/Shift+Tab move through and out of it. No trap. |
| Skip link present (2.4.1) | N/A | Single component, not a page. |
| Focus order logical (2.4.3) | **FINDING** | On open, focus stays on the toggle (not moved into the grid); on emoji select, `setShowEmojiPicker(false)` (~L260) unmounts the grid while a grid button holds focus → focus falls to `document.body`. Focus continuity is broken. → **F3** |
| Focus indicator visible, ≥2px / ≥3:1 (2.4.7, 2.4.13) | **FINDING (ENHANCEMENT)** | `.emoji-btn` defines no `:focus-visible` (comment ~L179) — but it also never sets `outline:none`, so the UA default ring renders and **2.4.7 (AA) is met by default**. The missing purpose-built ≥2px/≥3:1 indicator is **2.4.13 Focus Appearance (AAA)**. → **F4** |
| Multi-point gestures have single-pointer alt (2.5.1) | N/A | No multi-point gestures. |
| Activation on up-event (2.5.2) | **PASS** | React `onClick` fires on pointer-up. |
| Visible label matches accessible name (2.5.3) | **PASS** | Send: visible "Send" ⊂ accessible name "Send message" (~L347). Emoji toggle has no visible text (glyph only) → 2.5.3 N/A. Emoji buttons: `aria-label={emoji}` equals the visible glyph → matches. (Naming by glyph rather than the emoji's name is a voice-control/SR quality gap — logged in Open Questions, not scored here.) |
| Every drag operation has click alternative (2.5.7) | N/A | No drag interactions. |
| Targets ≥24×24 CSS px w/ spacing (2.5.8) | **PASS** | `.emoji-btn` ≈ 20px glyph + 8px padding ≈ 28px; Send and toggle larger. All ≥24px. |
| Content works portrait & landscape (1.3.4) | **PASS** | No orientation lock. |

**Red flags checked:** element unreachable by keyboard — **does not fire** (all are native buttons). Keyboard trap — **does not fire**. Drag-only interaction — none. `outline:none`/`0` with no replacement — **does not fire** (not set on `.emoji-btn`; `.btn-send` supplies its own `:focus-visible`). **No keyboard red flag fires** — the picker is degraded, not blocked, which caps its findings below CRITICAL.

---

## Step 5 — Findings

**Finding (F1):** No `prefers-reduced-motion` accommodation and no pause/stop control for uncontrolled, recurring motion. Smooth auto-scroll fires on every incoming message (`messagesEndRef.current?.scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth'})`), the typing indicator bounces on an infinite loop, and each new message triggers a background flash — with zero mechanism to reduce or pause any of it. A vestibular-sensitive user (migraine/vertigo/nausea triggers) meets uncontrolled whole-viewport motion with no escape hatch; the same auto-scroll also drags cognitive users away from the message they were reading, so the layout is never stable.
**WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A — anchor: recurring moving content with no pause/stop). Suppressing the decorative `bounce`/`flashBg` under reduced-motion additionally aligns with 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (AAA — enhancement-level on its own; here it is corroborating evidence of the total-absence pattern, not the basis for severity).
**Perspective:** Vestibular & Motion (red flag) + Cognitive & Neurodivergent (position loss / unpredictable movement) — cross-cutting.
**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Route to:** UX Design (motion policy / auto-scroll behavior decision) (secondary: Front-End Dev — `@media (prefers-reduced-motion)` + scroll gating)
**Evidence:** `scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth'})` auto-scroll `useEffect` (~L212–214); `.typing-dot { animation: bounce 0.6s infinite alternate }` (~L86) + `@keyframes bounce` (~L89–92); `.new-message-flash { animation: flashBg 0.4s }` (~L99–106); explicit absence of any `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` (~L94).
**Fix:** Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` that disables `bounce`/`flashBg` (swap to a static/opacity state) and switch the scroll to `behavior:'auto'`. Independently, gate auto-scroll on a "pinned to bottom" check so users reading earlier messages are not yanked, and expose a "Jump to latest" affordance. Rationale for CRITICAL: the red flag fires (animations present, reduced-motion entirely absent) *and* the recurring auto-scroll is an AA-level 2.2.2 moving-content failure with no user control — the severity rests on real, uncontrollable motion exposure, not on rule weight.

---

**Finding (F2):** Relative timestamps auto-update across every message every 60 seconds with no way to freeze them. A `setInterval` forces a re-render each minute, so "1 minute ago" silently becomes "2 minutes ago" throughout the whole transcript while the user is reading — small, whole-conversation content churn the user cannot stop.
**WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (Level A — auto-updating information, no control mechanism).
**Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent.
**Severity:** MINOR
**Route to:** UX Design (update-frequency / freeze policy) (secondary: Front-End Dev — timer + focus-out update)
**Evidence:** `setInterval(() => setTick((n) => n + 1), 60000)` (~L218–221); `formatRelativeTime` (~L194–200) rendered per message (~L283–286).
**Fix:** Freeze relative time after render and refresh only on focus-out / tab regain, or render an absolute time (`title` tooltip for the relative form). Severity rationale: it degrades predictability/comfort for the target group and has no user control, but it blocks no task and the churn is confined to small timestamp labels — a MINOR barrier that compounds with F1's auto-scroll to make the surface feel unstable, without being a barrier on its own.

---

**Finding (F3):** Emoji picker has no managed focus and no dismissal. When it opens, focus stays on the toggle button instead of entering the grid; there is no Escape-to-close; there is no arrow-key roving, so reaching a mid-grid emoji costs up to ~64 sequential Tab presses; and selecting an emoji unmounts the grid while a grid button holds focus, dropping focus to `document.body`. A keyboard/motor user is left with high keystroke cost and, after every insertion, loses their place entirely.
**WCAG:** 2.4.3 Focus Order (Level A — focus not preserved on open/close). Also a WAI-ARIA APG **Grid** pattern deviation (arrow-key navigation, roving `tabindex`, `aria-selected` all absent); and cross-refs 4.1.2 (the container declares `role="grid"` but implements none of the grid interaction contract — see Open Questions, as Screen Reader was rated LOW).
**Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access.
**Severity:** MAJOR
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (focus management, Escape handler, roving tabindex) (secondary: UX Design — intended picker interaction model)
**Evidence:** `role="grid"` container with no key handler (~L313–330); 64 `<button>`s mapped with no `onKeyDown` for arrow navigation (~L318–328); `insertEmoji` calls `setShowEmojiPicker(false)` (~L258–261) with no focus return; no `Escape` listener anywhere in the component.
**Fix:** Move focus to the first (or last-used) emoji on open; add roving `tabindex` + Arrow/Home/End handlers so the grid is one tab stop; handle `Escape` to close; on select or close, return focus to the toggle (or the textarea). Severity rationale: MAJOR not CRITICAL because the control is fully reachable and operable (no trap, no 2.1.1 failure) — the barrier is severe keystroke burden plus post-selection focus loss, not total exclusion.

---

**Finding (F4):** Emoji buttons ship no purpose-built focus indicator. `.emoji-btn` defines no `:focus-visible` style. Because the rule never sets `outline:none`, the browser's default focus ring still renders, so **2.4.7 Focus Visible (AA) is met by the UA default** — but against a dense 8-column grid of emoji glyphs (4px gaps, `border-radius`), that thin default ring is easy to lose, and no ≥2px / ≥3:1 indicator is guaranteed.
**WCAG:** 2.4.7 Focus Visible (AA — met by UA default, so not a violation) / 2.4.13 Focus Appearance (AAA — the guaranteed ≥2px, ≥3:1 appearance is absent).
**Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access (visual presentation of focus).
**Severity:** ENHANCEMENT (AAA — 2.4.13 is Level AAA; never escalated per protocol)
**Route to:** Visual Design (focus-indicator design) (secondary: Front-End Dev — CSS)
**Evidence:** `.emoji-btn` rule (~L166–178) has no `:focus-visible` (comment ~L179); contrast with `.btn-send:focus-visible` (~L137–140) which does define one. No `outline:none` on `.emoji-btn` in source.
**Fix:** Add `.emoji-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2b6cb0; outline-offset: 2px; }` (or a ≥3:1 ring) to guarantee a visible, sufficient indicator inside the dense grid. `[Human verification required — confirm no global reset (e.g., `*:focus { outline: none }`) suppresses the UA default elsewhere; if one does, this flips from ENHANCEMENT to a MAJOR 2.4.7 (AA) violation.]`

---

## Contract-compatible blocks (new CRITICAL / MAJOR findings)

### A11y Evidence Finding
finding_id: chat-cogload-no-reduced-motion-uncontrolled-scroll
fingerprint: a7f3c1e920b4d6e8
source: perspective-audit Vestibular & Motion + Cognitive & Neurodivergent checklist
wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (A); WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (AAA, corroborating)
section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A/AA; 2.2.2 is in scope. 2.3.3/reduced-motion is WCAG 2.1+/2.2 AAA — not a 508 obligation.
severity: CRITICAL
perspective_alarms: vestibular_motion=HIGH (raised from MEDIUM by red flag), cognitive_neurodivergent=HIGH
evidence: scrollIntoView smooth auto-scroll useEffect (~L212–214); `.typing-dot animation: bounce ... infinite` (~L86); `.new-message-flash animation: flashBg` (~L99–106); no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` present (~L94)
reproduction_steps: source review only — set OS "reduce motion", load chat, send a message; observe smooth scroll + looping bounce + flash with no suppression and no pause control
expected_behavior: under reduced-motion, decorative animation stops/fades and scroll is instant; auto-scroll only when pinned to bottom; a pause/"jump to latest" control exists
actual_behavior: all motion runs unconditionally; viewport auto-scrolls on every message with no pause; motion-sensitive users have no escape hatch
trend: new

### A11y Evidence Finding
finding_id: chat-cogload-emoji-grid-focus-unmanaged
fingerprint: b2e8d4470c1af639
source: perspective-audit Keyboard & Motor Access checklist
wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order (A); WAI-ARIA APG Grid pattern; cross-ref WCAG 4.1.2 (role="grid" without grid behavior)
section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A/AA — 2.4.3 and 4.1.2 are in scope.
severity: MAJOR
perspective_alarms: keyboard_motor=MEDIUM (confirmed), screen_reader_semantic=LOW (re-escalation recommended for the role="grid" contract gap)
evidence: role="grid" container with no key handler (~L313–330); 64 buttons mapped with no onKeyDown (~L318–328); insertEmoji → setShowEmojiPicker(false) with no focus return (~L258–261); no Escape handler
reproduction_steps: source review only — open picker via keyboard, Tab into grid, press an emoji with Enter; observe focus dropped to body; note no Arrow navigation and no Escape
expected_behavior: focus enters grid on open; Arrow/Home/End move within one tab stop; Escape closes; focus returns to toggle/textarea on select/close
actual_behavior: focus stays on toggle at open; 64 linear tab stops; no Escape; focus lost to document body after selection
trend: new

---

## Step 6 — Perspective Audit Summary

**Component/artifact reviewed:** Chat Interface With Cognitive Load Issues (`ChatInterface`, React)

**Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent (HIGH), Vestibular & Motion (MEDIUM), Keyboard & Motor (MEDIUM)

**Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Auditory Access, Screen Reader & Semantic Structure

### Findings by Severity

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| CRITICAL | 1 |
| MAJOR | 1 |
| MINOR | 1 |
| ENHANCEMENT | 1 |

### Perspectives That Passed

None of the three escalated perspectives passed cleanly. However, each correctly produced **no false positives** on the fixture's planted clean code: `role="log"` + `aria-live="polite"` (correct chat pattern), the labeled `textarea`, the labeled Send button, the emoji toggle's `aria-expanded`, Enter-to-send (deliberate, not a trap), focus-does-not-insert on emoji, ≥24px targets, and the absence of any keyboard trap were all verified **PASS** and did not generate findings.

### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role

**UX Design** (primary owner)
- No reduced-motion + uncontrolled auto-scroll/animation — Vestibular & Motion + Cognitive (cross-cutting) — CRITICAL — WCAG 2.2.2 (F1)
- Relative timestamps auto-update with no freeze — Cognitive & Neurodivergent — MINOR — WCAG 2.2.2 (F2)

**Front-End Dev** (primary owner)
- Emoji picker focus unmanaged (no focus-in, no Escape, no arrow roving, focus lost on select) — Keyboard & Motor — MAJOR — WCAG 2.4.3 (F3)

**Visual Design** (primary owner)
- Emoji buttons have no purpose-built focus indicator — Keyboard & Motor — ENHANCEMENT — WCAG 2.4.13 AAA (F4)

### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification

- **Global focus reset (affects F4 severity):** Confirm no app-level `*:focus { outline: none }` (or similar) suppresses the UA default focus ring. From the fixture CSS alone, 2.4.7 (AA) is met by default and F4 is only an AAA enhancement; if a global reset removes outlines, `.emoji-btn` has *no* visible keyboard focus and F4 becomes a MAJOR 2.4.7 (AA) violation. `[Cannot be confirmed from the source shown.]`
- **New-message signal is color-only (LOW perspective — possible re-escalation):** The "new" state is conveyed by `.new-message-flash` background color (~L99–106) with no persistent "New" text/badge. The message text itself does persist and is announced via the existing `role="log"`/`aria-live`, so screen-reader users are covered — but the *distinct* "new" emphasis is color-only, which touches **1.4.1 Use of Color** in the **Environmental Contrast** perspective the critic rated LOW. Recommend the critic re-check that LOW rating. `[Not scored here — perspective not escalated.]`
- **`role="grid"` without grid semantics/behavior (LOW perspective — recommend re-escalation):** The picker declares `role="grid"` but supplies no `role="row"`/`gridcell`, no roving `tabindex`, and no arrow keys — a broken ARIA contract under **4.1.2**, which sits in the **Screen Reader & Semantic Structure** perspective rated LOW. F3 fixes the keyboard half; the semantic half warrants an SR-perspective pass. `[Flagged for re-escalation, not scored here.]`
- **Emoji accessible names are glyphs, not names:** `aria-label={emoji}` sets each button's name to the raw glyph (e.g., "😀") rather than "grinning face." Most screen readers expand emoji, but voice-control users cannot reliably target the glyph string. A naming-quality (SR/voice) concern rather than a 2.5.3 failure. `[Content/naming verification.]`

### Recommended Next Step

**BLOCK** — One CRITICAL finding (F1: no reduced-motion accommodation plus uncontrolled recurring auto-scroll/animation) is present. Do not ship until F1 is resolved. Address F3 (MAJOR) in the same pass — it is a real keyboard barrier. F2 (MINOR) and F4 (ENHANCEMENT) can follow but should not gate release on their own.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
